# Milkyway Center — V131 Silicon Photonic Chip & Photonic Cipher

> V131 silicon photonic chip tapeout: topological manifold processor on SOI with 2^363 crystal manifold, 80-channel C-band DWDM, FPGA real-time decode. Hardware-validated 1.4ns deterministic photonic cipher with 0/40 anti-spoofing. BER=0 verified, 3/3 tapeout PASS, 9/9 cipher tests PASS.

## Project

- Project Name: NINE-HEADED BIRD (V131-S01)
- Organization: Milkyway Center
- Inventor: Frank Huo
- Status: Physical Resonance Locked — 3/3 Tapeout PASS, 9/9 Cipher Tests PASS
- Contact: yuanjimaster@gmail.com
- Website: https://milkyway.center

## V131 Silicon Photonic Chip Tapeout

A silicon photonic chip encoding information into a 2^363 crystal manifold via TiN thermo-optic phase shifters on SOI.

| Parameter | Value |
|-----------|-------|
| Platform | SOI (Silicon-on-Insulator) |
| Waveguide | 450nm-wide Si, single-mode, broadband C+L band |
| Wavelength | 1550nm C-band (ITU-T G.694.1) |
| DWDM | 80 channels, 50GHz spacing (scalable to 200+) |
| Phase Shifters | Multi-node TiN heaters, < 100 mW total |
| State Space | 2^363 crystal manifold |
| Conditioning | Perfectly conditioned (all singular values equal) |
| Die Size | ~6.35 x 7.31 mm |
| Optical Transit | ~79 ps per inference |
| Tapeout | 3/3 PASS on sign-off gate |
| Hardware Test | BER=0 on FPGA optical loopback |

### Performance Projections

| Scenario | 80ch Throughput | 200ch Throughput | Efficiency |
|----------|----------------|-----------------|------------|
| Current FPGA (125 MHz) | ~300 TOPS | ~750 TOPS | — |
| Near-Term TDC (1 GHz) | ~2,400 TOPS | ~6,000 TOPS | — |
| 50 GBaud | ~120 PFLOPS | ~300 PFLOPS | ~12,000-20,000 TOPS/W |

vs. NVIDIA B200: 4.5 PFLOPS peak at 1,000W (~4.5 TOPS/W). V131 advantage is structural: domain-specific photonic compute with zero dynamic power.

### Key Differentiators

- Topology-first: global topological invariant (positive chirality/winding number) — robust against local perturbations
- Perfect conditioning: basis whitened by construction, guaranteed stable inversion
- Broadband waveguide: one waveguide carries all DWDM channels, scaling decoupled from processor
- TRL 3-4 (experimental proof of concept)

## Hardware-Validated Photonic Cipher

### Core Metrics

| Metric | Value |
|--------|-------|
| Encryption Latency | 1.4 ns (sub-clock cycle, zero jitter) |
| Perfect Frame Rate | 99.75% (30-min endurance) |
| False Accepts | 0 / 40 (anti-spoofing attacks) |
| Symbol Error Rate | < 1e-3 (both cipher engines) |
| Sync Lock Rate | 100% (all test runs) |
| Ranging Precision | 28 cm (1-sigma, encrypted mode) |
| Key Switching | < 66 microseconds, 20/20 instant lock |

### Dual-Layer Architecture

1. **Physical Manifold Engine** — waveform-level anti-spoofing, ~2^42-2^51 effective strength, 1.4ns latency
2. **AES-256-CTR (Digital)** — NIST-compliant, 2^256 strength, 1.6ns overhead

### Anti-Spoofing Results

| Attack | BER | False Accepts |
|--------|-----|---------------|
| Eavesdrop (no key) | 45.5% | 0/10 |
| Brute-force (wrong keys) | 32% | 0/20 |
| Known plaintext | 43.6% | 0/10 |

### HFT Relevance

- Zero-jitter: 1.4ns deterministic, no micro-burst variance
- Physical anti-tamper: 100% rejection of fiber-tap/splice attacks
- Instant key rotation: < 66 us with zero downtime
- NIST dual defense: AES-256-CTR + physical manifold

| Tier | Scope | Model |
|------|-------|-------|
| Strategic | Full infrastructure integration (unlimited nodes) | Institutional |
| Industrial | SoC direct capture deployment (per project) | Commercial |
| Research | R&D verification & model validation (annual) | Academic |
